steps.title: Focus on relative heat
steps.content: The student's goal is to emphasize how hot the Sun is compared with nearby stars. The notes show that most nearby stars in the study were cooler than the Sun, while a smaller group was hotter.

steps.title: Rule out incomplete uses
steps.content: Choice B lists classifications without making the comparison. Choice C mentions only the cooler K and M stars. Choice D groups several star classes together and hides the hot-versus-cool relationship.

steps.title: Choose the full comparison
steps.content: Saying the Sun is hotter than most but not all nearby stars uses the temperature data accurately and keeps the focus on the Sun's relative heat. The answer is A.
